explanation: TRUE POSITIVE. The validateTx function in tx_pool.go lacks an explicit IsPrague check before accepting TxTypeEthereumSetCode transactions. The existing type checks at lines 706 and 710 only gate on IsEthTxType (EIP-1559 activation), which is already active. The pool.signer is always a pragueSigner (set via LatestSignerForChainID at line 264), so ValidateSender succeeds for SetCode txs regardless of fork status. This allows SetCode transactions to enter the mempool before Prague activation. At block execution time, these txs are rejected by MakeSigner returning a londonSigner (which can't handle SetCode), but critically no gas is charged since failure occurs before ApplyMessage. An attacker can thus flood the tx pool with SetCode transactions at minimal cost — their balance passes pool checks but is never actually deducted — consuming pool resources and potentially displacing legitimate transactions. Both Cypress and Kairos configs have PragueCompatibleBlock: nil, confirming the vulnerability window exists on live networks. The fix is straightforward: add `if !pool.rules.IsPrague && tx.Type() == types.TxTypeEthereumSetCode { return ErrTxTypeNotSupported }` in validateTx.
